scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked) = 2, scorable. The 2 diffs are the outer-beqz delay-slot fill + the displaced store. Full-build SHA1 is the real gate.

sessions.lever: blocked-lane triage — 3 more rules-removed full-build variants (incl. branch-sense flip), all DIFF

reviewer.evidence: No improving candidate — candidate.c is HEAD's body (matches only with the 1 rule). No new cheat.

remaining_gap.summary: cc1-internal delay-slot fill choice. Target eager-fills the outer `beqz v0` (the `(u32)t0 < 2` test) delay slot with `andi v0,a2,0x10` (the then-block's first insn), keeping `sh a1,8(v1)` before the branch. cc1's reorg.c::fill_simple_delay_slots instead moves `sh a1,8(v1)` DOWN into the slot. Confirmed cc1-internal (raw cc1 output before maspsx already mis-fills).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: func_80078A68 in regfix.txt:2935 `reorder 12,11 @11-12`. Must go for COMPLETED-C. No pure-C source found that flips cc1's delay-slot heuristic to eager-fill from the then-block.

next_hypotheses: DIRECTED PERMUTER (the named untried avenue) — PERM_* on this single function from the clean (rule-removed) base. Random masked search won't help; needs the delay-slot structural seed. Mirror functions func_80078B3C / func_80078B70 share this exact fill choice; func_80078B04 (same file) was a 7-session wall finally closed 2026-06-12 (commit 03c07363) — check what lever closed B04 and whether it transfers.

Make `sh a1,8(v1)` unmovable into the slot: present arg1's store value so cc1 can't legally hoist the store past the branch into the delay slot (e.g. a data dependency that ties the store to a post-branch value), forcing eager-fill from the then-block. Must not change emitted bytes elsewhere.

Read cc1 reorg.c fill_simple_delay_slots / fill_eager_delay_slots: identify the exact tiebreak (probably eligible-insn count / probability) and find a C shape that makes the then-block insn the only fill candidate.
